problem: How many ways are there to put 5 balls in 3 boxes if the balls are not distinguishable but the boxes are?

classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: What is the difference between the balls being distinguishable and not distinguishable in this problem?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Why do we only need to count the number of balls in each box if the balls are indistinguishable?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many ways are there to arrange the balls in the boxes if all 5 balls are in the same box?
classified_questions.label: b
